slides.kicker: // sample template · logistics and supply chain
slides.lead: Demo (sample) template presentation. All text is dummy and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, five views on the same goal.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Current flows, data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Orders, telematics and warehouse scans capture more than anyone can read. Value appears only when they connect.
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: fewer late deliveries after route optimization
saved daily on manual stock reconciliation
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: what matters is the effect on flow.
Start where you already have data and feel the pain most.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a caption, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not wait for a stockout, it predicts it.
bullets: Orders, lead times and seasonality across SKUs.
What preceded past shortages and surpluses.
Before a shelf runs empty or a yard overflows.
Replenishment into the window that costs least.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same network, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: We expedite only when a line goes empty. Costly and unpredictable.
Replenish on a fixed calendar. Better, but often too early.
Replenish on real demand signals. Lowest total cost.
Critical SKUs predictive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 card grid. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a smart supply chain.
cards: Scans and telematics in real time, close to the source.
Live view of shipment status and exceptions.
Flags unusual delays before a person notices.
From raw data straight to a daily ops briefing.
Data and routing stay under your control.
A team that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Gains slow down, it pays to finish the basics.
Tools improve fast and reshape routine planning.
Automation also designs the routing, not just the math.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: The winner is not the one with the most data, but the one who turns it into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every tool has the name of a person who stands behind it.
You know in advance how you will tell it worked.
On critical steps a person always decides.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.h1: A report from data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build a daily briefing live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
